sources.claim: WCAG 2.2 is the normative accessibility basis for focus, target size, color-independent meaning, and other accessibility requirements.
W3C WAI explains focus appearance as ensuring keyboard focus indicators are clearly visible and discernible.
sources.source: W3C Understanding Success Criterion 2.4.13 Focus Appearance

sources.claim: Visual design should be tested using methods such as 5-second testing, first-click testing, and preference testing.
sources.source: Nielsen Norman Group, Testing Visual Design: A Comprehensive Guide

sources.claim: Progress indicators should show real-time status of a process.
Material Design accessibility guidance links visual hierarchy and structure to accessible comprehension.
